title: Percent, Rates, Markups, Discounts, and Repeated Change · Decision Defense
summary: Audit prices, discounts, taxes, tips, population changes, and performance claims. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a consumer percent-claim audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify the comparison base before calculating a percent, percentage, or whole.
Calculate absolute and relative change and explain when each comparison is useful.
Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A price of $120 is reduced by 18%. What is the new price?
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use 120 × (1 − 18/100), then compare the result with the original price.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use 120 × (1 − 18/100), then compare the result with the original price.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Percent Claims applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Detect denominator switching, percentage-point confusion, and selective baselines in public claims.

hints: Name the Percent Claims precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
